content:
Hey, I've been messing around with rust and linux for quite some time and want to start contributing to the kernel with rust. I know there is already an open thread with this question, but there are some other things that i need some help. I'm having a hard time finding where to contribute mostly due to not knowing how you contribute to drivers that you can't use or are not needed on your devices. How do you do it? Also, I'd really like to just deep dive into a driver and just contribute to it as much as I can until there is nothing to do XD, any recommendations? In terms of hardware i just have my laptop with an intel cpu and nvidia pascal gpu.

Thanks in advance :) .

One possibility I usually suggest is subscribing to the mailing list and reading and reviewing other patches -- that helps a lot, gives you credit, and by doing so you will eventually spot code and areas you could help with (and that interest you more etc.).

But, yeah, as you mentions, we have some other threads with other ideas and links to issue lists that can help you get started. For instance, please see this recent one at: #Help > Looking for open issue @ 💬.

I hope that helps :)
Ok, i will start looking into some mails in the mailing list and reviewing. But if i dont have the hardware, how do i test a driver? Is testing it in a VM enough?
You don't have to test driver to start contributing. You can start helping by testing/reviewing the kernel library changes (under $srctree/rust).
I don't think there is a reliable method of testing hardware driver without having the hardware.
There is always qemu. But eventually you probably want real hardware.
